name: WIMPless dark matter
summary: A class of models — emerging naturally from gauge-mediated SUSY breaking — in which dark matter has the correct thermal relic density without weak-scale mass or weak interactions. The mass m_X and coupling g_X scale together so that g_X^4/m_X^2 ~ G_F^2 m_W^2, reproducing the WIMP miracle for any (m_X, g_X) along the curve. Allows DM masses from 10 MeV to 10 TeV.

addresses_puzzles.note: Generalises the WIMP miracle to a scaling relation; fits sub-GeV DM, hidden-sector DM, and asymmetric variants under one umbrella. Makes contact with hidden-sector phenomenology (dark photon, dark Higgs).

touches_benchmarks.note: Hidden-sector DM with feeble coupling typically falls below LZ sensitivity; sub-GeV WIMPless candidates are targeted by SuperCDMS / SENSEI / DAMIC at low recoil.
WIMPless models are commonly mediated by dark photons with kinetic mixing; predicted signals at NA64 / BaBar / Belle II.

new_symmetries: dark gauge or global symmetry stabilising X
introduces.note: DM mass, mediator mass, hidden coupling, kinetic mixing.

history: Feng and Kumar (2008), 'The WIMPless Miracle: Dark-Matter Particles without Weak-Scale Masses or Weak Interactions', PRL 101, 231301, arXiv:0803.4196. Generalised the WIMP-miracle reasoning beyond the EW scale; led directly to systematic studies of sub-GeV thermal DM through 2010s-2020s.

references.citation: Feng and Kumar, 'The WIMPless Miracle: Dark-Matter Particles without Weak-Scale Masses or Weak Interactions', PRL 101, 231301 (2008)
